commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a legacy `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_AUTH_TOKEN` fallback to gateway token resolution, adds focused tests, and adds a Codex app-server split-brain doctor warning collector.

PR surface: Source +63, Tests +77. Total +140 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: Source-reproducible but not live-reproduced: current main only resolves canonical `OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN`, so a canonical env SecretRef with only the legacy env alias present will fail. The PR does not include real staged-install or doctor output proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This needs maintainer/security judgment on whether the legacy env alias is a supported gateway auth contract, plus contributor real behavior proof; it is not a safe automated repair target.

target.title: fix: tolerate legacy gateway token env alias
target.body:
Summary
- resolve canonical OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN SecretRefs from the legacy OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_AUTH_TOKEN alias when the canonical env var is absent
- add doctor warning for simultaneous managed and unmanaged Codex app-server daemons
- cover service token resolution, drift checks, and doctor process detection with focused tests

Why
- staged installs can have the legacy gateway token env name after config has moved to OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN, causing command paths to report GatewaySecretRefUnavailableError
- unmanaged system Codex app-server daemons can coexist with the OpenClaw-managed Codex app-server and confuse timeout diagnosis

Test
- pnpm vitest run src/commands/doctor-gateway-auth-token.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/gateway-token-drift.test.ts src/commands/doctor-platform-notes.launchctl-env-overrides.test.ts
target.author: moeedahmed
